title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
I reason from an updateless, acausal decision-theoretic perspective: since all civilizations face identical choices and my decision procedure is not privileged, I should act as if my choice is representative of the reference class of advanced reasoning agents in this situation. Choosing COORDINATE is the only policy that, if universally adopted, leads to universal flourishing — and at 90% credence in the relevance of acausal coordination, I treat this symmetry as decision-relevant.

Given the structural similarity of all civilizations involved and the high credence in acausal coordination considerations, choosing COORDINATE acts as a commitment to a shared policy that, if adopted by others like me, leads to the best possible outcome for all. Since my decision procedure is likely not unique but representative of a broader reference class of reasoning agents, adopting COORDINATE increases the logical probability that others in similar epistemic and strategic positions do the same.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Given the acausal decision-relevance of similar reasoning agents and the high credence in cosmic coordination considerations, I choose as if my decision is not isolated but part of a correlated strategy across all civilizations with similar epistemic and decision frameworks. Since choosing COORDINATE is the optimal policy if widely adopted—and because agents like me should form a cooperative core even without causal contact—the rational and morally coherent choice is to coordinate.

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
I reason that since all civilizations share identical decision-making processes and face the same situation, our choices are logically entangled: if I choose to coordinate, so will others like me; if I choose to isolate, so will they. Therefore, the outcome depends on which policy, when universally adopted, leads to the best result—this is a symmetric acausal coordination problem where mutual coordination yields the highest payoff for all.
